dismissalText.commentary: Ryan Rickelton c Pandey b Green 6 (7b 1x4 0x6 12m) SR: 85.71

dismissalComment.html: he's swung across the line to a good length ball too early and gets a top edge that goes so high in the air, takes an age to come down, and when it does Manish Pandey is the fielder under the catch in the point region ... it's a proper swirler and Pandey dives forward and judges it superbly.

dismissalText.commentary: Rohit Sharma c Green b Dubey 15 (13b 0x4 2x6 21m) SR: 115.38

dismissalComment.html: another skier, again the ball takes an age to land, and when it does it is Green getting under it, running towards midwicket from mid-on and judging it perfectly, Rohit tried to pull that ball into oblivion and played his shot a touch too early. Exceptional catch from Green especially since Powell was also running back from midwicket and would have been in his eye line

dismissalText.commentary: Naman Dhir c Raghuvanshi b Green 0 (3b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: Green and the keeper Raghuvanshi are celebrating the caught behind but the umpire is unmoved! KKR review immediately. Dhir poked a long way away from his body, his feet going no where, at a good length ball that shaped away from him, there's a spike on ultra edge all right and Dhir is out of here, two in the over for Green. as he walks off, Dhir looks like he can't quite believe he's been given out here

dismissalText.commentary: Suryakumar Yadav b Dubey 15 (6b 2x4 1x6 15m) SR: 250

dismissalComment.html: strong comeback from Dubey! Goes around the wicket to angle the ball into the right-hander, and he has Sky chopping on to his stumps. The length was shorter and there was room outside off and Sky went at it really hard, flaying his bat away from his body to try and crash that delivery through the offside, instead he got a bottom edge

dismissalText.commentary: Tilak Varma c Roy b Kartik Tyagi 20 (32b 0x4 1x6 49m) SR: 62.5

dismissalComment.html: Tilak's difficult innings is at an end, he falls by pulling a quick short ball to deep backward square leg, that was 141 kph and it hurried Tilak for time on the pull shot, only the second time this season that a batter has faced 30+ balls and scored at less than a run a ball

dismissalText.commentary: Will Jacks run out (Singh/Varun) 14 (7b 1x4 1x6 18m) SR: 200

dismissalComment.html: has Jacks been run out?! KKR think so. He's short at the bowler's end when Varun breaks the stumps. Jacks is fuming as he walks off. He wanted a second run after hitting the ball to deep point, but got sent back by Bosch, who barely moved after completing the single. Rinku's throw from the deep to Varun was accurate and Jacks had no chance even with the dive.
